backlog.title: Harness System Integration Bugfix
backlog.description: End-to-end validation and repair of the Harness System (PRD §7) integration bugs delivered in plan/004_9a50e71828f4. Four in-scope issues: (1) CRITICAL — configureHarnesses() global default never reaches the Agent, so default/no-harness Agent construction throws 'Harness anthropic is not registered'; (2) MAJOR — PiHarness ignores the toolExecutor callback, making Agent-configured tools invisible/non-executable (PRD §7.10); (3) MINOR — streaming metadata.provider reports the harness id instead of the LLM provider; (4) MINOR — registerDefaultHarnesses() is not exported from the public barrel and never auto-invoked (compounds Issue 1). Issue 5 (unicode workflow names) is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE. Success criterion: the shipped example examples/harnesses/02-harness-configuration.ts and the PRD §7.6 quickstart (configureHarnesses + new Agent({model})) work end-to-end, and the full npm test suite is green.

milestones.title: Critical — Default/No-Harness Agent Construction (Issues 1 + 4)
milestones.description: Deliver the documented primary user journey: constructing an Agent that relies on the global default harness (the exact pattern in PRD §7.6/§7.7, docs, and example 02) must work. Issue 1 rewires the Agent's harness resolution from the legacy getGlobalProviderConfig singleton (default 'anthropic') to the new getGlobalHarnessConfig/resolveHarnessConfig cascade (default 'pi'). Issue 4 adds the registration safety net (export registerDefaultHarnesses + lazy auto-register) so the resolved 'pi' harness actually exists in the registry with zero user setup. These two issues are coupled: the PRD states 'even after the cascade fix, new Agent({ model }) will fail unless the user has registered a pi harness somewhere.' Together they make scenario 1 (global 'pi' default, no explicit harness) and scenario 4 (nothing configured) both succeed.

tasks.title: Rewire Agent harness resolution to the new cascade (Issue 1)
tasks.description: Replace the legacy harness resolution in src/core/agent.ts (getGlobalProviderConfig + resolveProviderConfig, which read the wrong singleton defaulting to 'anthropic') with the new cascade utilities (getGlobalHarnessConfig + resolveHarnessConfig, defaulting to 'pi') at all three call sites: the constructor, stream(), and executePrompt(). Remove the now-dead legacy import. Preserve the 'is not registered' substring contract so existing regex-based throw assertions still match. This is the root-cause fix for the Critical bug.

subtasks.title: Rewire all three harness-resolution call sites in agent.ts to getGlobalHarnessConfig/resolveHarnessConfig and remove the legacy import
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_9a50e71828f4/bugfix/001_a8d42149bc87/architecture/issue1-agent-harness-resolution.md and system_context.md §2. The Agent reads the WRONG singleton: getGlobalProviderConfig() returns globalProviderConfig (default {defaultProvider:'anthropic'}, a SEPARATE store from globalHarnessConfig). configureHarnesses() writes globalHarnessConfig, which the Agent never reads for harness resolution. Three call sites in src/core/agent.ts must be rewired:
   - CONSTRUCTOR (lines 117-120): `const globalConfig = getGlobalProviderConfig(); const resolved = resolveProviderConfig(globalConfig, this.harnessId, this.harnessOptions); const effectiveHarness = resolved.provider;` → swap to getGlobalHarnessConfig()/resolveHarnessConfig(), destructure `.harness` not `.provider`. The constructor throws at line 128: `throw new Error(\`Harness '${effectiveHarness}' is not registered\`)` — PRESERVE the literal substring 'is not registered'.
   - stream() (lines 372-378): `const globalConfig = getGlobalProviderConfig(); const { provider: resolvedHarness, options: resolvedHarnessOptions } = resolveProviderConfig(globalConfig, this.harnessId, this.harnessOptions, promptHarness, promptHarnessOptions);` → swap to getGlobalHarnessConfig()/resolveHarnessConfig() passing the SAME 5 args (global, agentHarness, agentOptions, promptHarness, promptOptions), destructure `.harness`/`.options`. It throws synchronously at line 390 before generator creation — PRESERVE 'is not registered'.
   - executePrompt() (lines 616-622): same pattern as stream(); returns createErrorResponse('PROVIDER_NOT_FOUND', ...) at line 633 (NOT a throw) — keep that error-return behavior, only change the resolution functions.
2. INPUT: src/utils/harness-config.ts exports getGlobalHarnessConfig() (returns globalHarnessConfig ?? DEFAULT_HARNESS_CONFIG where DEFAULT_HARNESS_CONFIG = {defaultHarness:'pi'}) and resolveHarnessConfig(globalConfig, agentHarness?, agentOptions?, promptHarness?, promptOptions?) → {harness, options} — a PURE function performing NO id validation (verified). getGlobalHarnessConfig is ALREADY imported in agent.ts at line 46; you must ALSO import resolveHarnessConfig from '../utils/harness-config.js' and REMOVE the legacy import of {resolveProviderConfig, getGlobalProviderConfig} from '../utils/provider-config.js' at line 45.
3. LOGIC: This is a near drop-in swap. The shape is identical: resolveHarnessConfig returns {harness, options} (vs legacy {provider, options}). Update destructuring names accordingly (provider→harness). Do NOT touch the model axis at line 661 — it already correctly reads getGlobalHarnessConfig().defaultModelProvider. Do NOT touch this.harnessId assignment at line 111 (the config.harness ?? config.provider as HarnessId fallback stays — provider:'claude-code' must keep resolving, since 'claude-code' is a valid registry key; only unregistered 'anthropic' literals will throw, which is the intended post-migration behavior).
4. OUTPUT: agent.ts compiles (npm run lint = tsc --noEmit passes). After the swap, getGlobalProviderConfig() must have ZERO remaining call sites in agent.ts (grep to confirm). Run the FULL `npm test` suite — most tests will still pass (tests using provider:'claude-code' resolve correctly since 'claude-code' is registered; tests registering their own mock 'anthropic' harness like src/__tests__/unit/agent.test.ts still work because they explicitly register 'anthropic'). If any OTHER test file breaks incidentally due to the legacy 'anthropic' default no longer being auto-applied, update that test's mock registration to match the new reality (the cascade now defaults to 'pi'). Do NOT modify Issue 5's edge-case.test.ts (out of scope). Document any test you change in the commit.
5. MOCKING: No external services. The cascade utilities are pure functions. Tests reset the registry via HarnessRegistry._resetForTesting() and resetGlobalHarnessConfig()/resetGlobalConfig() in beforeEach/afterEach — preserve those.
6. VERIFICATION: `grep -n 'getGlobalProviderConfig\|resolveProviderConfig' src/core/agent.ts` returns nothing. `npm run lint` green. `npm test` green except the known out-of-scope edge-case.test.ts unicode failure.

subtasks.title: Add regression test: configureHarnesses({defaultHarness:'pi'}) + new Agent() (no harness) resolves to 'pi' — covers PRD cascade scenario 1
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/test-patterns-and-conventions.md §3 and §8. NO existing test covers the default-resolution path through the NEW configureHarnesses()/getGlobalHarnessConfig() singleton at the Agent constructor level. The only existing 'no harness' test (src/__tests__/unit/agent.test.ts ~L127-141) exercises the LEGACY provider cascade (registers a mock 'anthropic' harness) — do NOT modify it (it guards the legacy regression contract); add a SIBLING test. This gap is the root cause of why Issue 1 slipped through.
2. INPUT: From P1.M1.T1.S1, the Agent constructor now reads getGlobalHarnessConfig() (default 'pi') instead of the legacy singleton. registerDefaultHarnesses() (src/harnesses/register-defaults.ts) and HarnessRegistry are available. resetGlobalHarnessConfig() exists in harness-config.ts for test isolation.
3. LOGIC: Add a new test (e.g. in src/__tests__/unit/agent.test.ts under a new describe block, or a new file src/__tests__/unit/agent-default-harness-resolution.test.ts). The test MUST: (a) reset registry + global config in beforeEach/afterEach (HarnessRegistry._resetForTesting(); resetGlobalHarnessConfig(); vi.clearAllMocks()); (b) call configureHarnesses({defaultHarness:'pi', defaultModelProvider:'anthropic'}); (c) register the real 'pi' harness OR a mock 'pi' harness via HarnessRegistry.getInstance().register(...); (d) construct `new Agent({ model: 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514' })` with NO harness field; (e) assert it does NOT throw and resolves to the 'pi' harness (assert agent.harness.id === 'pi' or equivalent accessor). Also add the inverse assertion: without configureHarnesses and with the registry holding only a mock 'claude-code', new Agent() with no harness resolves to 'pi' only if 'pi' is registered (this documents the 'first-defined-wins / global default' cascade per PRD §7.7). Mirror the proven test idiom: import {describe,it,expect,vi,beforeEach,afterEach} from 'vitest'; relative imports with .js extensions; @ts-expect-error for private field access.
4. OUTPUT: A passing test that FAILS on the pre-fix code (proving it guards the regression) and PASSES after P1.M1.T1.S1. This test isolates Issue 1 (it manually registers 'pi', so it does NOT depend on Issue 4's auto-register).
5. MOCKING: Mock the harness via createMockHarness('pi') factory (see agent-prompt-harness-override.test.ts:33-72 pattern) or register a real PiHarness. No SDK calls occur at construction time.

subtasks.title: Add the 4-scenario cascade matrix regression test at the Agent level (global / agent / prompt combinations)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/test-patterns-and-conventions.md §8. There is NO single test co-locating the 4 canonical cascade scenarios at the Agent level. The pure-function matrix is partially covered in src/__tests__/unit/utils/harness-config.test.ts (describe('cascade integration') ~L377-405) but NOT through the Agent constructor. The PRD's 4 reproduction scenarios (the cascade matrix table in h3.0) are the acceptance contract.
2. INPUT: From P1.M1.T1.S1 (Agent reads new cascade) and P1.M1.T2.S1 (defaults auto-register / are available). resolveHarnessConfig cascade priority: promptHarness ?? agentHarness ?? globalConfig.defaultHarness (first-defined-wins); options merge last-write-wins (globalDefaults ← agentOptions ← promptOptions).
3. LOGIC: Add a parametrized/table test (vitest it.each or 4 explicit its) asserting the resolved harness for: (1) global='pi', no agent harness, no prompt harness → 'pi'; (2) global='pi', agent harness='claude-code', no prompt → 'claude-code'; (3) global='pi', agent='claude-code', prompt='pi' → 'pi' (prompt wins); (4) NOTHING configured (no configureHarnesses, no agent harness, no prompt) → 'pi' via the default + the auto-registration safety net from P1.M1.T2.S1. Assert via the Agent constructor (scenario 1-3) and via Agent.prompt()/Agent.stream() harness override for the prompt-axis cases (mirror src/__tests__/unit/agent-prompt-harness-override.test.ts and agent-stream-harness-override.test.ts idioms). Scenario 4 specifically validates the FULL no-setup journey and therefore REQUIRES P1.M1.T2.S1 — register mocks for the other scenarios but leave scenario 4 with zero manual registration to prove the safety net.
4. OUTPUT: 4 passing test cases. Scenarios 1-3 must FAIL on pre-fix code (proving regression value); scenario 4 additionally proves Issue 4. All pass after P1.M1.T1.S1 + P1.M1.T2.S1.
5. MOCKING: Use createMockHarness for 'pi' and 'claude-code' where explicit registration is needed. Reset registry + global config in beforeEach/afterEach.

tasks.title: Default harness registration safety net — export registerDefaultHarnesses and lazy auto-registration (Issue 4)
tasks.description: The registry starts EMPTY (HarnessRegistry.getInstance() does not auto-register). configureHarnesses({defaultHarness:'pi'}) makes the cascade resolve 'pi', but registry.get('pi') returns undefined unless the user manually registers. Deliver the PRD §7.1/§7.6 convenience: export registerDefaultHarnesses from the public barrel src/index.ts AND add a guarded lazy auto-registration so new Agent({model}) works with zero setup. This is the safety net that makes Issue 1's fix deliver the documented UX end-to-end.

subtasks.title: Export registerDefaultHarnesses from src/index.ts and implement guarded lazy auto-registration of the built-in 'pi' + 'claude-code' harnesses
End-to-end regression test — new Agent({model}) with ZERO setup works; smoke-test example 02 launch
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/system_context.md §3 (the documented primary user journey) and issue1-agent-harness-resolution.md §8 (example 02 reproducer). examples/harnesses/02-harness-configuration.ts calls configureHarnesses({defaultHarness:'pi'}) at L70-71, manually registers both harnesses at L98-99, then constructs new Agent({name:'DefaultAgent'}) at L121 with no harness — currently crashes 'Harness anthropic is not registered'. The PRD minimal repro (h3.0) is even stricter: configureHarnesses({defaultHarness:'pi'}) + new Agent({model}) with NO manual registration must work.
2. INPUT: From P1.M1.T1.S1 (Agent reads new cascade) + P1.M1.T2.S1 (public export + lazy auto-register).
3. LOGIC: Add a test that constructs `new Agent({ model: 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514' })` with: NO configureHarnesses call, NO registerDefaultHarnesses call, NO manual registry.register — in a freshly-reset registry (HarnessRegistry._resetForTesting() in beforeEach). Assert it does NOT throw and resolves to a 'pi' harness (proving the full Issue 1 + Issue 4 journey). Additionally add a smoke test that imports and exercises the example 02 module path OR replicates its exact sequence (configureHarnesses + the documented quickstart) and asserts no throw. If the examples/__tests__ glob is configured (vitest.config.ts includes examples/__tests__/**/*.test.tsx), place an example-launch assertion there; otherwise replicate the sequence in src/__tests__/.
4. OUTPUT: A test proving the zero-setup journey works — the ultimate acceptance criterion for Milestone M1. Must FAIL on pre-fix code (throws 'Harness anthropic is not registered') and PASS after both dependencies land.
5. MOCKING: For the constructor-only assertion, no SDK mocking needed (construction does not call the SDK). If exercising execute/stream, mock the Pi SDK via the makeFakeSession pattern (architecture/test-patterns-and-conventions.md §6).

milestones.title: Major — PiHarness toolExecutor Wiring (Issue 2)
milestones.description: PiHarness.execute()/executeStreaming() declare a toolExecutor parameter (the Agent.toolExecutor bridge to the Agent's MCPHandler) but never invoke it — it is a dead parameter. Tools are dispatched exclusively through the harness's OWN internal MCPHandler.registered.executor, which the Agent never populates for the Agent-driven path. Result: any tool configured at the Agent level (config.mcps, config.tools, or request.options.tools) is invisible and non-executable by PiHarness, directly violating PRD §7.10 and the P2.M3.T1.S1 contract. This milestone wires the caller-supplied toolExecutor into PiHarness's tool dispatch so Agent-configured tools round-trip through the real bridge.

tasks.title: Wire toolExecutor into PiHarness tool dispatch (PRD §7.10)
tasks.description: Make PiHarness's customTools dispatch through the caller-supplied toolExecutor callback instead of (or in safe addition to) the harness's internal registered.executor. Add a ToolExecutionResult-aware converter to MCPHandler, thread toolExecutor into buildCustomTools, rebind each tool's execute to invoke it, and update both call sites.

subtasks.title: Add toAgentToolResultFromExecResult converter to MCPHandler (reads ToolExecutionResult.content/.isError, not blind stringify)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/issue2-piharness-toolexecutor.md Finding 7. The existing MCPHandler.toAgentToolResult(result: unknown, isError: boolean) at src/core/mcp-handler.ts L272-281 blindly stringifies whatever it receives (`const text = typeof result === 'string' ? result : JSON.stringify(result)`). This is WRONG for the toolExecutor path: the caller's toolExecutor returns a ToolExecutionResult {content: string|unknown, isError: boolean} (src/types/harnesses.ts L145-150). If reused, it would JSON.stringify the entire {content,isError} object into one text chunk rather than emitting the semantic content.
2. INPUT: ToolExecutionResult interface (types/harnesses.ts L145-150): { content: string | unknown; isError: boolean }. AgentToolResult shape returned by toAgentToolResult: { content: [{type:'text', text}], details: {isError} }.
3. LOGIC: Add a new private/public method to MCPHandler (src/core/mcp-handler.ts), e.g. `toAgentToolResultFromExecResult(result: ToolExecutionResult): AgentToolResult<{isError:boolean}>`. It must: read result.content; if content is a string, emit {type:'text', text: content}; if content is unknown/object, JSON.stringify it (mirror toAgentToolResult's stringification ONLY for the content field, not the whole envelope); set details.isError = result.isError. Do NOT modify toAgentToolResult itself (the ClaudeCodeHarness path via toAgentSDKServer still uses it and must keep working). Import the ToolExecutionResult type with `import type`.
4. OUTPUT: A new converter method on MCPHandler. Unit test it directly: pass {content:'hello', isError:false} → expect content[0].text==='hello' && details.isError===false; pass {content:{foo:1}, isError:true} → expect JSON-stringified content && details.isError===true.
5. MOCKING: Pure function — no external services.

subtasks.title: Thread toolExecutor into PiHarness.buildCustomTools and rebind each tool's execute to invoke it; update both call sites
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/issue2-piharness-toolexecutor.md Findings 1-6. In src/harnesses/pi-harness.ts: buildCustomTools() (L649-661) is currently `return this.mcpHandler.toPiCustomTools();` — zero-arg, no rebinding. It is called at L250 (non-streaming execute) and L370 (streaming executeStreaming); both pass the result directly as `customTools` to createAgentSession. The toolExecutor parameter is declared in execute() (L214) and executeStreaming() (L350) but NEVER invoked (grep confirms 3 hits, all signature/forward; zero call sites). MCPHandler.toPiCustomTools() (mcp-handler.ts L236-261) binds each tool's execute to registered.executor (L253) — the harness's OWN executor, not the caller's.
2. INPUT: From P1.M2.T1.S1, MCPHandler has toAgentToolResultFromExecResult(result: ToolExecutionResult). The caller's toolExecutor signature: (req: ToolExecutionRequest) => Promise<ToolExecutionResult> where ToolExecutionRequest = {name:string, input:unknown} (types/harnesses.ts L127-131). Pi's per-tool execute signature (mcp-handler.ts L246-256): async (_toolCallId, params, _signal, _onUpdate, _ctx) => Promise<AgentToolResult<{isError}>>.
3. LOGIC: (a) Change buildCustomTools signature to accept toolExecutor: `private buildCustomTools(toolExecutor: (req: ToolExecutionRequest) => Promise<ToolExecutionResult>): ToolDefinition[]`. (b) RECOMMENDED approach (preserves existing pi-harness-customtools.test.ts): add an OPTIONAL toolExecutor parameter to MCPHandler.toPiCustomTools(toolExecutor?) — when provided, rebind each tool's execute to: `const res = await toolExecutor({ name: fullName, input: params }); return this.toAgentToolResultFromExecResult(res);` (use fullName, the map key already in scope at mcp-handler.ts L237, as the tool name; pass params as input). When toolExecutor is undefined, fall back to the current registered.executor behavior (backward compat). (c) buildCustomTools then becomes `return this.mcpHandler.toPiCustomTools(toolExecutor);`. (d) Update BOTH call sites: L250 `customTools: this.buildCustomTools(toolExecutor)` and L370 `customTools: this.buildCustomTools(toolExecutor)` (toolExecutor is in scope in both execute() and executeStreaming() as a parameter). The PRD frames this as 'PiHarness ignores the toolExecutor callback' — the caller's toolExecutor becomes the dispatch target for the Pi path. Do NOT touch ClaudeCodeHarness (it uses toAgentSDKServer, unaffected) or the skills/resourceLoader path.
4. OUTPUT: PiHarness customTools now dispatch through the caller's toolExecutor. `grep toolExecutor src/harnesses/pi-harness.ts` shows it threaded into buildCustomTools at both call sites. npm run lint + npm test green (existing pi-harness-customtools.test.ts still passes because toPiCustomTools() with no arg falls back to current behavior — verify the existing test calls buildCustomTools() with no arg OR update it to pass a toolExecutor; prefer keeping the fallback so the existing test is untouched).
5. MOCKING: In tests, the Pi SDK is mocked via private-field overwrite (makeFakeSession). The toolExecutor can be a vi.fn() capturing {name, input}.

subtasks.title: Regression test — PiHarness tool call invokes toolExecutor with {name, input} and returns its result (PRD §7.10 contract)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/issue2-piharness-toolexecutor.md Finding 8. The existing src/__tests__/unit/providers/pi-harness-customtools.test.ts passes an executor arg to execute() but NEVER asserts it is called (the fake session.prompt is a no-op that never triggers a tool call). PRD §7.10 is therefore untested — this is why Issue 2 slipped through. The PRD Steps to Reproduce (h3.1) shows the exact expected round-trip: register an inprocess tool 'srv__ping', pass an agentToolExecutor, and when the Pi session emits a tool call the toolExecutor must be invoked with {name:'srv__ping', input:{x:'val'}} and its result returned.
2. INPUT: From P1.M2.T1.S2, PiHarness customTools dispatch through toolExecutor. The Pi SDK mock pattern (makeFakeSession + scripted events) from architecture/test-patterns-and-conventions.md §6: use piTurnEndTool(toolName, args, usage) / piToolExecStart / piToolExecEnd helpers (harness-parity.test.ts:132-170) to script a tool-call turn, OR capture the customTools passed to createAgentSession (via pi.sdk.createAgentSession.mock.calls[0][0].customTools) and directly invoke customTools[0].execute(toolCallId, params, ...) to assert the round-trip.
3. LOGIC: Add a test (extend pi-harness-customtools.test.ts or new file) that: (a) constructs a real PiHarness, initializes it, overwrites sdk via the makeFakeSession/wirePi pattern; (b) registers an inprocess tool via harness.registerMCPs([{name:'srv', transport:'inprocess', tools:[{name:'ping', description:'d', input_schema:{type:'object',properties:{x:{type:'string'}}} }]}]); (c) defines `const agentToolExecutor = vi.fn(async (req) => ({ content: 'agent-' + req.name, isError: false }))`; (d) calls harness.execute({prompt:'hi', options:{}}, agentToolExecutor); (e) EITHER scripts a piTurnEndTool that triggers the tool, OR directly invokes the captured customTools[0].execute('tc1', {x:'val'}, undefined, undefined, {}); (f) asserts agentToolExecutor was called with { name: 'srv__ping', input: {x:'val'} } (expect(agentToolExecutor).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.objectContaining({name:'srv__ping', input:{x:'val'}}))); (g) asserts the returned AgentToolResult has content[0].text === 'agent-srv__ping' and details.isError === false (proving the toAgentToolResultFromExecResult converter was used). The pre-fix code would print '[agentToolExecutor CALLED]' never and raise 'No executor registered for inprocess tool srv__ping'.
4. OUTPUT: A test that FAILS on pre-fix code (toolExecutor never called / 'No executor registered' error) and PASSES after P1.M2.T1.S2. This directly asserts the PRD §7.10 contract.
5. MOCKING: Pi SDK via makeFakeSession/wirePi. agentToolExecutor is a vi.fn().

milestones.title: Minor — Streaming Metadata Provider Semantics (Issue 3)
milestones.description: The streaming metadata StreamEvent reports the harness id as provider (pi-harness emits 'pi', claude-code-harness emits 'claude-code') instead of the resolved LLM provider (e.g. 'anthropic'). Both harnesses set `provider: this.id`. While shape-parity holds (PRD §7.14.4), the value is semantically wrong per PRD §7.8 ('the harness is never part of the model string' / harness ⊥ provider). Fix both emission sites to use modelSpec.provider, and update the two value-asserting tests that will break.

tasks.title: Fix metadata.provider conflation in both harnesses and update the two affected tests
tasks.description: One-token semantic fix at both streaming metadata emission sites (this.id → modelSpec.provider), plus updating the two existing tests that assert the old (wrong) harness-id values.

subtasks.title: Change metadata.provider from this.id to modelSpec.provider in PiHarness + ClaudeCodeHarness; update the two breaking value assertions
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/issue3-4-metadata-and-exports.md (Issue 3 section). Two emission sites set `provider: this.id`:
   - src/harnesses/pi-harness.ts L374-382 (inside executeStreaming): `yield { type:'metadata', metadata: { requestId: \`${this.id}-${Date.now()}\`, model: modelSpec.model, provider: this.id } }`. modelSpec is in scope at L358 (`const modelSpec = this.normalizeModel(request.options.model ?? 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514')`).
   - src/harnesses/claude-code-harness.ts L696-704 (inside executeStreaming): same shape, `provider: this.id`. modelSpec in scope at L666-669.
   StreamEvent type (src/types/streaming.ts L41): { type:'metadata'; metadata:{ requestId?:string; model?:string; provider:string } } — provider is the only REQUIRED field. ModelSpec.provider (types/harnesses.ts L229-238) is the correct LLM host (ModelProviderId).
2. INPUT: modelSpec is already computed and in scope at both sites. No new imports needed (modelSpec is a local const).
3. LOGIC: At BOTH sites, change `provider: this.id` → `provider: modelSpec.provider`. Leave model: modelSpec.model unchanged (already correct). Do not touch requestId (it intentionally includes this.id).
4. OUTPUT: Streaming metadata now reports the LLM provider. CRITICAL — TWO EXISTING TESTS ASSERT THE OLD VALUE AND MUST BE UPDATED (verified via grep):
   - src/__tests__/unit/providers/pi-harness-streaming.test.ts:210: `expect(events[0].metadata.provider).toBe('pi');` → change to the modelSpec.provider the test's model resolves to (e.g. 'anthropic' if the test uses a default/anthropic model — inspect the test's input model and the normalizeModel output to set the correct expected value).
   - src/__tests__/unit/providers/claude-code-harness-execute.test.ts:1218: `expect(events[0].metadata.provider).toBe('claude-code');` → change to the resolved modelSpec.provider (e.g. 'anthropic').
   Inspect each test's actual model input to compute the precise expected provider value before updating (do not assume 'anthropic' — read the test fixture). Run `grep -rn "metadata.provider\|metadata: { provider" src/__tests__/` after the edit to confirm no other assertion references the old harness-id values.
5. MOCKING: None beyond the existing Pi/Anthropic SDK mocks already in those test files.
6. VERIFICATION: npm run lint + npm test green. The two updated tests pass with modelSpec.provider values.
